North Side Gang.description: Multi-ethnic criminal organization in Chicago during Prohibition, founded in 1919 by Dean O'Banion and Hymie Weiss. It controlled bootlegging, gambling, and robbery on Chicago's North Side, and was the principal rival of Al Capone's Chicago Outfit. The gang was active from 1919 to 1935.

Escritório do Crime.description: Brazilian militia of hitmen and elite killers operating in the west zone of Rio de Janeiro, founded in 2007. It engages in illegal real estate activities such as land grabbing, construction, and property sales/rentals, with its main activity being contract killings. The group is composed of military police officers and former police, led by ex-BOPE captain Adriano Nóbrega and PM major Ronald Paulo Alves Pereira. It is suspected of involvement in 19 unsolved homicides since 2002, including the assassination of councilwoman Marielle Franco.

'Ndrina Pelle.description: The Pelle 'ndrina, also known as Pelle-Vottari, is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in San Luca, Italy. It is among the most influential 'ndrine, with members holding key positions in the organization's consultative and top bodies, particularly in the Ionian mandate and the province. The clan is active in international cocaine trafficking, with contacts with Colombian cartels, as well as in contract rigging and extortion. It was documented in the 2010 Reale operation as having influence in regional election campaigns.

Katcha dacoits.description: Criminal gangs operating in the riverine areas of Sindh and Punjab, Pakistan, involved in kidnapping, extortion, murder, dacoity, banditry, smuggling, arms trafficking, and highway robberies. Originating from local feudalism in the 1950s-1960s, they have over 20,000 members and use sophisticated weaponry. Despite multiple operations, they remain active and influential.

'Ndrina Carpino.description: A 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Petronà, Cerva, Sersale, and the Levante area of Calabria, Italy. It operates in Liguria, particularly in Chiavari and Lavagna. The clan was involved in the Faida di Petronà, a series of murders and attacks starting in 1992. In 2014, assets worth €2 million were seized from an alleged affiliate.

'Ndrina Gentile.description: The 'Ndrina Gentile is a mafia clan ('ndrina) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Amantea, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in extortion, drug trafficking, and usury. The clan has been subject to multiple police operations, including Operation Nepetia in 2007 which led to 39 arrests. It has also been linked to political corruption, notably supporting regional councilor Franco La Rupa.

Sergi 'ndrina.description: The 'Ndrina Sergi is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Calabrian mafia organization, based in Platì, Italy. It is involved in drug trafficking, particularly cocaine and heroin, operating in northern Italy (Milan, Busto Arsizio, Gallarate) and Rome, with a strong presence in Australia since the 1970s. The clan has been implicated in kidnappings, murders, and political corruption, and has faced numerous arrests and operations against it.

Primeiro Comando da Capital.description: Brazilian organized crime syndicate founded on August 31, 1993, by eight prisoners at Taubaté Penitentiary in São Paulo. It is Latin America's biggest drug gang with 40,000 lifetime members and 60,000 contractors as of 2023. The group is based in São Paulo and operates throughout Brazil, South America, West Africa, and Europe, with a focus on cocaine trafficking. It has partnered with the Italian 'Ndrangheta and controls over 50% of Brazil's drug exports to Europe.
